data.body: Subagent review (2026-06-16) across convergence/maintainability/reliability/fitness/hygiene/correctness. Verdict: strong plan; applied these fixes: (1) CORRECTNESS — corrected the tomllib AC: requires-python is ALREADY >=3.11 (pyproject:16), so tomllib needs no fallback/dep; the py310 is only the ruff LINT target (pyproject:105). (2) Added a testable fail-closed AC (preserve verify-gate: present-but-unreadable=>require sig). (3) Noted config is working-tree content on main, NOT events on the tickets branch (no event-log migration). (4) Concretized back-compat (old config reads identically >=1 release; unknown keys warn->error). (5) Flagged REBAR_<KEY> as a breaking rename of unprefixed public vars (~40 vars / ~150 sites) -> spun into a separate linked story (60ce). (6) Added explicit 'retire both inline parsers' AC. (7) Added platformdirs exclusion (hand-rolled XDG, stdlib-only). (8) Module-size: extend the 55-LOC config.py, keep parity with/retire the rebar-config.sh mirror. DECOMPOSED into 7 ordered child tasks (744e ADR -> 252e Config -> {43a0 loader, fe78 route+retire+fail-closed, c647 show-config} -> 83e6 back-compat -> f81a docs) + the separate env story 60ce.
